title: GTA VI will launch at 80 euros, will have better performance on PS5, and some stores require physical disc
summary: Rockstar's long-awaited title confirms its price and performance exclusivity, while retailers refuse to sell it without physical format.

title: Figma incorporates animations, code layers, and new AI features in its Config 2026
summary: The platform designs a suite of tools that combine motion, code, and third-party artificial intelligence to improve collaboration.

title: Google launches new AI features, security, and reduces Play Store commissions
summary: The tech giant updates its services with computer control via Gemini, biometric identification at airports, and changes to its store.

title: Meta accelerates AI amid criticism: mass layoffs, high executive compensation, and privacy concerns
summary: Zuckerberg's company pushes AI features while employees warn about risks and job cuts and large stock options are revealed.

title: Controversies over state surveillance: mass espionage, data extraction, and predictive algorithms
summary: Several governments are accused of expanding their surveillance capabilities through legally and ethically questionable practices.

title: OpenAI unveils 'Jalapeño', its first custom AI chip in collaboration with Broadcom
summary: The new processor is designed to optimize the company's models and reduce its dependence on external suppliers.

title: Micron breaks records and signs deals through 2030, boosting memory stocks
summary: The memory chip manufacturer records extraordinary profits and secures 16 supply deals that boost the sector.

title: US reinforces anti-China semiconductor bloc by including Netherlands and investing in xLight
summary: The Pax Silica alliance expands as Washington pressures Europe with $150 million for the EUV light source.

title: Heat wave in Europe causes hospital emergencies and urges urban adaptation solutions
summary: Extreme high temperatures overwhelm hospitals in England and lead European cities to redesign their infrastructures.

title: OpenAI introduces ads in paid plans and faces a lawsuit in Florida
summary: The artificial intelligence company implements advertising in its plans while the state of Florida sues it for hiding risks of ChatGPT.

title: Anthropic accuses Alibaba of extracting abilities from its Claude model using fake accounts
summary: The AI startup reports that Alibaba used nearly 25,000 accounts to clone its technology.

title: IBM presents the world's first chip with sub-nanometer technology
summary: The new processor marks a milestone in semiconductor miniaturization and promises greater efficiency.

title: A24's $75 million deal with Google enrages its fans
summary: The independent studio yields to Google's artificial intelligence in a pact that fans consider a betrayal.

title: Elon Musk loses trillionaire status after SpaceX stock drop
summary: The businessman's fortune shrinks due to the decline in his aerospace company's valuation.

title: Unusual double earthquake shakes Venezuela and Android phones alert the population
summary: The earthquake releases a century's worth of energy in 39 seconds, while smartphones help warn citizens.

title: Microsoft acknowledges that 8 GB of RAM is enough for Windows 11 and launches Surface with that configuration
summary: After years of recommending 16 GB, the company admits its operating system works well with half and markets devices accordingly.

title: Google continues to lose star artificial intelligence researchers to competitors
summary: The talent drain in Google's AI teams intensifies in the face of offers from rivals.

title: Data centers under scrutiny for high water and energy consumption
summary: Drought in Georgia, Polaroid campaigns, and the UN demand greater environmental responsibility from data centers.

title: Chinese artificial intelligence attracts investments but worries its own experts
summary: Morgan Stanley doubles humanoid robot forecasts while Chinese scientists warn about the risks of AI.

title: White House helps Zuckerberg and Google CEO avoid subpoena, and its app installs without permission
summary: The US government faces criticism for pressuring the Senate and for the forced download of its app on official phones.
